_README.instructions: All other languages copy this file and translate the values. Keys (left of the colon) and identifiers (like worksheet codes 'w01') must never change. See translator-guide.html for full instructions.

header._comment: Top of the page — the big title block.
header.title: Energy & Water Worksheets
header.lede: Interactive teaching materials on efficient use of energy and water — created by five European vocational schools.

ui._comment: Small bits of interface text used across the page.

topicFilter._comment: Buttons at the top of the worksheet grid. Emoji is part of the label — keep it.

levels._comment: Three difficulty levels. Each has a short title and a longer description.

basic.label: Basic
basic.title: Observe & Measure
basic.desc: Hands-on data collection. Students walk through a building, observe, measure, and record. Minimal calculations, focus on understanding what uses energy and water.

medium.label: Medium
medium.title: Calculate & Compare
medium.desc: Apply formulas to real data. Students use measurements to calculate costs, savings, and payback periods. Combine data from multiple sources.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.title: Analyze & Recommend
advanced.desc: Synthesize findings into recommendations. Students analyze trade-offs, create cost-benefit analyses, and produce reports or videos with actionable proposals.

countries._comment: Country names that appear on each worksheet card.

worksheets._comment: Every card on the landing page. Keys (w01, w02 ...) never change. Topic, title and description translate.

w01.title: 💧 Water — Tap Flow Measurement
w01.desc: Measure actual tap flow rates in school bathrooms. Compare with automatic sensor taps and calculate water savings.

w02.title: 📺 Standby vs. OFF
w02.desc: Measure standby power of TVs, consoles and monitors. Calculate the yearly cost of leaving devices plugged in.

w03.title: 🍽 Dishwasher Efficiency
w03.desc: Compare intensive, auto and eco programmes. Calculate annual energy, water and carbon impact of running a dishwasher.

w04.title: 🔌 Kitchen Appliance Inventory
w04.desc: List all kitchen appliances, read their power ratings, and rank them by energy consumption.

w05.title: 🧺 Eco Settings Experiment
w05.desc: Compare water and energy use of normal vs. eco wash cycle on a washing machine. Record and compare.

w06.title: 💡 Bulb Comparison Lab
w06.desc: Compare incandescent, CFL, and LED bulbs side by side. Measure light output, heat, and power consumption.

w07.title: 🖥 Computer Lab Energy Audit
w07.desc: Measure power draw of computers, monitors, and projectors. Calculate daily and yearly cost for a full lab.

w08.title: 📊 Energy Rating Comparison
w08.desc: Compare energy classes (A–G) for TVs and monitors. Calculate the real cost difference over 5 years.

w09.title: 🍞 Oven vs. Air Fryer Showdown
w09.desc: Compare energy used to cook the same meal in a traditional oven vs. air fryer vs. microwave.

w10.title: 🌡 Dryer Energy Calculator
w10.desc: Calculate yearly cost of using a tumble dryer vs. air-drying. Factor in weather and frequency.

w11.title: 💰 The Cost of Neglect
w11.desc: Calculate the actual yearly cost of leaving lights on unnecessarily in different rooms of a school or home.

w12.title: ☀️ Solar Panel Calculator
w12.desc: Use PVGIS data to calculate solar panel output for your school. Compare with grid electricity cost and calculate ROI.

w13.title: 🏠 Smart Home Energy Plan
w13.desc: Design an energy-saving plan for a household. Prioritize upgrades by payback period and total savings.

w14.title: ♨️ Water Heating Analysis
w14.desc: Compare the efficiency of different water heating methods (kettle, boiler, heat pump). Calculate theoretical vs. actual energy input.

w15.title: 🤖 Robot vs. Human: Total Cost
w15.desc: Analyze the full energy lifecycle of a robotic vacuum vs. traditional cleaning. Include manufacturing, use, and disposal.

w16.title: 🧠 Smart Lighting Proposal
w16.desc: Design a smart lighting system for a school. Include sensors, timers, and dimming. Create a cost-benefit report.

footnote._comment: Box at the bottom of the page, before the footer.
footnote.title: 📌 About these worksheets
footnote.p1: These worksheets are part of GREEN Erasmus+ (KA220-VET), Work Package 2: Creation of teaching materials on efficient use of energy and water. Five partner schools each lead one topic area, creating materials in all partner languages.
footnote.p2: Difficulty levels are designed so teachers can choose appropriate worksheets for their students — from hands-on observation (Basic) through calculation exercises (Medium) to full analysis and recommendation projects (Advanced).
